mri: Balanced steady-state free precession (bSSFP / FIESTA / TrueFISP)
ultrasound: Focused Assessment with Sonography for Trauma (FAST)
nuclear_medicine: Indium-111 white blood cell scan (WBC scan)

barium: Barium sulfate suspension (Readi-Cat, Volumen, E-Z-Cat, Baro-Cat)

vascular_access: Tunneled central venous catheter (Hickman, Broviac, Groshong)
embolization: Uterine artery embolization (UAE) / uterine fibroid embolization (UFE)
Portal vein embolization (PVE) — preoperative liver hypertrophy
musculoskeletal: Epidural steroid injection (ESI) — interlaminar, transforaminal, caudal
Radiofrequency ablation of nerves (medial branch / genicular)
venous: Deep vein thrombosis (DVT) treatment — CDT, pharmacomechanical

size_measurement: RECIST criteria (Response Evaluation Criteria in Solid Tumors)
abdominal_pelvic: Cavernous transformation of the portal vein
neuroimaging: Dense MCA sign (hyperdense middle cerebral artery)
musculoskeletal: Pelvic fracture (open book, lateral compression, vertical shear)
Meniscal tear (horizontal, vertical, radial, bucket-handle, complex)
SLAP tear (superior labrum anterior to posterior)

categories.3: Probably benign — <2% malignancy risk, short-interval follow-up

categories.LR-M: Probably or definitely malignant, not HCC specific

categories.1: Very low (clinically significant cancer highly unlikely)
categories.5: Very high (clinically significant cancer highly likely)

categories.TR3: Mildly suspicious (3 points) — FNA if ≥2.5 cm
categories.TR4: Moderately suspicious (4-6 points) — FNA if ≥1.5 cm
categories.TR5: Highly suspicious (≥7 points) — FNA if ≥1 cm

categories.I: Simple cyst — thin wall, no septa, no calcification, no enhancement
categories.II: Minimally complex — thin septa, fine calcification, <3 cm hyperdense cyst
categories.IIF: Minimally complex requiring follow-up — more septa, thicker calcification
categories.III: Indeterminate — thick/irregular septa or wall, measurable enhancement
categories.IV: Cystic renal malignancy — enhancing soft tissue component

imaging_modalities.FAST: focused assessment with sonography for trauma
imaging_modalities.MBS / VFSS: modified barium swallow / videofluoroscopic swallow study

mri_sequences.TrueFISP: true fast imaging with steady-state precession
mri_sequences.HASTE: half-Fourier acquisition single-shot turbo spin echo

interventional_radiology.UAE / UFE: uterine artery embolization / uterine fibroid embolization

technical_equipment.DICOM: digital imaging and communications in medicine

reporting.NAD: no acute disease / no abnormality detected

thoracic: Pulmonary arteries (main, right, left, lobar, segmental)
Pulmonary veins (right superior, right inferior, left superior, left inferior)
abdominal_aorta_branches: Replaced left hepatic artery (from left gastric)

findings_phrases: No evidence of acute pulmonary embolism
recommendation_phrases: Further evaluation with MRI is recommended
PET/CT may be considered for further evaluation
No further imaging follow-up is needed per Fleischner Society guidelines
If clinically indicated, further workup is suggested
This is a critical / emergent finding and has been communicated to [clinician]
critical_results: Findings were communicated to Dr. [name] by [method] at [time]

scanner_parameters: Kernel / reconstruction filter (soft tissue, bone, lung)
systems_informatics: DICOM (digital imaging and communications in medicine)

histology: Renal cell carcinoma (clear cell, papillary, chromophobe)

head_brain: Ventricles are normal in size and configuration
No restricted diffusion to suggest acute infarction
Mastoid air cells and paranasal sinuses are clear
Flow voids in the major intracranial arteries are preserved
chest: The cardiomediastinal silhouette is within normal limits
Lungs are clear without focal consolidation, pleural effusion, or pneumothorax
The thoracic aorta is normal in caliber
Support devices are in satisfactory position (lines / tubes)
ET tube tip is approximately X cm above the carina
Central venous catheter tip is in the cavoatrial junction
NG tube tip is in the stomach
abdomen_pelvis: The liver is normal in size and attenuation
The gallbladder is unremarkable without cholelithiasis
The spleen is normal in size
The kidneys enhance and excrete contrast symmetrically
The abdominal aorta is normal in caliber
The bowel is unremarkable without obstruction
The uterus and adnexa are unremarkable (female)
The prostate is unremarkable in size (male)
spine: The conus medullaris terminates at a normal level
vascular: No evidence of deep venous thrombosis
Veins are compressible with normal augmentation
